description: Upgrade phase 05 handover from a markdown summary to a visually polished PDF customer deliverable (pandoc + weasyprint, four embedded charts, cover page, colour-coded roadmap table). Simultaneously resolves all four open change requests: add Incident Response and Compliance services to the MSP portfolio, add a build/buy/partner decision gate for no-known-service gaps, harden the intake budget validation, and generalise assumption revalidation to all phase HITL gates.

userStories.title: Add weasyprint dependency to pyproject.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need weasyprint installed as a project dependency so the PDF render step works out of the box after a standard pip install.
acceptanceCriteria: weasyprint added to [project].dependencies in pyproject.toml
pip install -e . installs weasyprint successfully
python3 -c 'import weasyprint' exits 0 after install
Existing pytest suite still passes
ruff and mypy pass

userStories.title: Extend RSK schema with likelihood and impact fields

acceptanceCriteria: RSK block schema in the relevant orchestrator prompt (03-mapping.md) gains two required fields: likelihood: low | medium | high and impact: low | medium | high
Validator updated: any RSK block missing likelihood or impact produces a validation error

Meridian-Logistics/_risks.md backfilled: RSK-001 (NIS2 deadline) likelihood:high impact:high; RSK-002 (patch gap) likelihood:medium impact:high; RSK-003 (WAN capacity) likelihood:medium impact:medium; RSK-004 (budget overrun) likelihood:high impact:medium; RSK-005 (TISAX timeline) likelihood:medium impact:high
Validator runs green on updated Meridian-Logistics engagement (python -m validator Meridian-Logistics or equivalent)
pytest passes

userStories.title: Python chart generator — four charts from engagement registers
userStories.description: As the harness, I need a deterministic Python module that reads an engagement's markdown registers and produces four matplotlib PNG charts for embedding in the handover PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: Module at harness/charts.py exposes generate_charts(engagement_path: Path) -> dict[str, Path]
Chart 1 — Spend stacked bar: Y1/Y2/Y3 columns with stacked capex/opex segments; data from RMI blocks in 04-roadmap/roadmap.md grouped by year
Chart 2 — Gantt swim-lane: one row per RMI item grouped by phase (foundations/enablers/value-drivers/optimisations), shaded by year bucket; data from 04-roadmap/roadmap.md
Chart 3 — Risk status donut: segments open/mitigated/accepted/closed; data from _risks.md RSK blocks

Chart 4 — Gap coverage bar: one horizontal bar per GAP block coloured by mapping outcome (fully-mapped/partially-mapped/no-known-service); data joined from 02-gap/gaps.md and 03-mapping/service-map.md
Charts written to <engagement_path>/05-handover/charts/ as spend_bar.png, gantt.png, risk_donut.png, gap_coverage.png
If a source register is absent, that chart is skipped with a logged warning; other charts still render
generate_charts(Path('Meridian-Logistics')) completes without error and produces four non-empty PNGs
pytest includes at least one test per chart verifying the PNG is produced and file size > 0 using Meridian-Logistics fixtures
ruff and mypy pass

userStories.title: HTML/CSS pandoc template and MSP logo placeholder
userStories.description: As the harness, I need an HTML/CSS template that pandoc uses to produce a visually polished PDF with a cover page, MSP branding, colour-coded roadmap table, and chart image embedding.
acceptanceCriteria: Template at assets/handover-template.html is a valid pandoc HTML5 template
MSP logo placeholder at assets/msp-logo.png exists (any non-empty PNG; add a comment near the template noting where to replace it)

CSS provides: Inter or system-sans font stack; cover page block with MSP logo, $customer-name$ variable, engagement date, and title; colour-coded roadmap table (Y1 one accent colour, Y2 second, Y3 third via CSS classes); quick-win badge styling; section headings with colour accent; chart images sized to page width via max-width:100%
Running pandoc --template assets/handover-template.html on Meridian-Logistics/05-handover/handover.md produces an HTML file without errors
ruff and mypy pass (no Python changes required in this story)

userStories.title: PDF render post-step in phase 05 harness
userStories.description: As a consultant, I want phase 05 to automatically produce handover.pdf after the markdown is ratified, so I have a ready-to-send customer deliverable with no manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: harness/enter_phase.py (or equivalent phase runner) gains a post-ratification hook triggered only for phase 05-handover
Hook step 1: calls generate_charts(engagement_path) from harness/charts.py
Hook step 2: runs pandoc <handover.md> --template assets/handover-template.html with chart image paths passed as metadata variables, writing intermediate HTML
Hook step 3: runs weasyprint <handover.html> <handover.pdf>
Hook step 4: logs the absolute path of the produced PDF
05-handover/handover.pdf is present in Meridian-Logistics engagement after hook runs
PDF file size > 1 KB
If weasyprint or pandoc is not installed, hook raises RuntimeError with clear installation instructions
pytest passes
ruff and mypy pass

userStories.title: Add Compliance domain and five compliance services to MSP portfolio
userStories.description: As the mapping phase, I need a Compliance service domain so regulatory and framework-related gaps map to real services instead of [no-known-service].
acceptanceCriteria: ~/msp-portfolio/Compliance/ directory created with five service files: NIS2_Entity_Classification.md, TISAX_Readiness.md, ISMS_Programme.md, GDPR_Gap_Assessment.md, Regulatory_Deadline_Tracking.md
Each file follows the established format (YAML frontmatter + markdown body with Overview, What's Included, What's Not Included, Dependencies)
NIS2_Entity_Classification covers: scoping Essential vs Important entity status, regulatory deadline tracking, Art.21 and Art.23 obligations
TISAX_Readiness covers: label scoping, gap assessment against VDA ISA, readiness roadmap for automotive suppliers
ISMS_Programme covers: ISO 27001 scope definition, risk treatment plan, control implementation, certification readiness
GDPR_Gap_Assessment covers: data mapping, lawful basis review, DPIA support, breach notification readiness
Regulatory_Deadline_Tracking covers: ongoing monitoring of applicable regulatory deadlines with proactive advisory alerts
Portfolio MCP search_services('NIS2') returns NIS2_Entity_Classification
Portfolio MCP search_services('TISAX') returns TISAX_Readiness
Portfolio MCP search_services('ISO 27001') returns ISMS_Programme
Portfolio MCP list_services_in_domain('Compliance') returns all five services
All five files committed to ~/msp-portfolio/ with message: feat: add Compliance domain with 5 services

userStories.title: Build/buy/partner HITL decision gate for [no-known-service] gaps
userStories.description: As a consultant, I want to be forced to make a documented build/buy/partner decision for any gap that maps to [no-known-service], so the roadmap always has a resolution path and the decision is recorded as a DEC-NNN entry.
acceptanceCriteria: prompts/orchestrator/03-mapping.md updated: when synthesizer produces a MAP entry with [no-known-service], it must also emit a DECISION_REQUIRED block listing the gap ID, gap description, and three options (build in-house / buy third-party product / partner with specialist firm)
HITL Gate 1 checklist in 03-mapping.md gains item: All [no-known-service] MAP entries have a corresponding DEC-NNN decision in _decisions.md selecting build/buy/partner with rationale
Phase 03 ratification is explicitly blocked until all [no-known-service] entries have a resolution decision in _decisions.md
Synthesizer prompt instructs that the roadmap must include a sourcing/procurement RMI item for each [no-known-service] gap resolved as buy or partner
ruff and mypy pass
pytest passes

userStories.title: Budget validation hard gate at intake (CR-2026-005)
userStories.description: As a consultant, I want intake to flag and require explicit sign-off when the customer's stated budget envelope is likely undersized for their stated goals, so budget gaps are surfaced before scope is fixed.
acceptanceCriteria: prompts/orchestrator/00-intake.md updated with a budget-complexity estimate step: after goals and scope are captured, synthesizer produces a rough order-of-magnitude cost range based on goal count, compliance framework count, infrastructure domain count, and greenfield vs brownfield indicator

If estimated range exceeds stated envelope by more than 25%, synthesizer emits a BUDGET_GAP_WARNING block containing: stated envelope, estimated range, gap percentage, and prompt for consultant to choose (a) revise envelope, (b) reduce scope, or (c) proceed with documented risk acceptance
HITL gate in 00-intake.md gains mandatory checklist item: If a BUDGET_GAP_WARNING was raised, a DEC-NNN decision recording the resolution choice is present in _decisions.md
Phase 00 may not ratify scope.md until budget gate is cleared: either no warning raised, or warning raised and DEC-NNN recorded
backlog/change-requests/CR-2026-005.md status updated to shipped
ruff and mypy pass
pytest passes

userStories.title: Generalise assumption revalidation gate to all phases (CR-2026-006)
userStories.description: As a consultant, I want every phase HITL gate to enforce that all assumptions flagged requires_revalidation:true targeting that phase are formally resolved before ratification, so _assumptions.md is accurate at engagement close.
acceptanceCriteria: Assumption schema doc (or templates/assumption.template.md) gains three new optional fields: resolution_status: pending | resolved | deferred; resolved_in_phase: phase identifier; resolution_note: free text

Each of the six orchestrator prompts (00-intake through 05-handover) has its HITL gate checklist updated with: All assumptions in _assumptions.md with requires_revalidation:true and target_phase matching this phase have resolution_status:resolved or resolution_status:deferred with a non-empty resolution_note

03-mapping.md specifically updated per CR-2026-006: HITL Gate 1 checklist item added and service-map template gains an Open Assumptions Resolution section listing unresolved assumptions targeting this phase

Meridian-Logistics/_assumptions.md backfilled: ASM-002 given resolution_status:deferred, resolved_in_phase:03-mapping, and resolution_note explaining the deferral (MSP delivery capacity not formally validated but accepted given engagement scope)

Validator updated: any assumption with requires_revalidation:true and a non-empty target_phase must have a non-empty resolution_status field; missing resolution_status on such assumptions produces a validation error
Validator runs green on updated Meridian-Logistics engagement
backlog/change-requests/CR-2026-006.md status updated to shipped
pytest passes
ruff and mypy pass
